title: Asukas new test plugsuit
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's new test plugsuit.             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Tue Nov 16, 2010 4:41 am At my homeforum, some guy has started a thread where he tries to review Asuka's new plugsuit and build theories around it's purpose. Others joined the "brainstorm". Some of the theories are: 1. Transparent material in the suit is used to lower a synchrorate. (As we know, it is lower with no suit at all.) This is due to Eva-03's untested status and for safety measures. Lowering the synchrorate can also be explained as a reasonable sufficiency for a first-time launch to test some of the basic nerve signals' feedback. But an author of this theory didn't explain why transparency of a material affects a syncrorate, even though it is still impenetrable to LCL. Or does it have to be transparent in order to be penetrable. 2. Transparent material is used as a mean of visual observation of the pilot's body for abnormalities. First, what kind of abnormalities could emerge and why would someone expect them? Second, how was someone going to observe Asuka's back when it's pressed against the throne? Third, what about other parts of the body? No answers were given. Some unanswered questions can be applied to both previous theories. Why this kind of test plugsuit was never used before? Eva-03 is not the only one which undergone it's first launch. What's so special about it that requires a new plugsuit? Eva-06 is more advanced, but still, Kaworu didn't wear a gay plugsuit. 3. This theory is pure genius. Too bad it was created for lulz and trololo. Here it goes. It seems, Angel was residing in the core of Eva-03. How did he turn up inside the entry plug? Wait a minute... Plug suit, of course! Rei was eaten by Zeruel, but never got infected. Asuka did. Why? Plug suit! It all makes sense now. In fact it IS Bardiel. This son of a ***** is like a cacoon around victims body. This plug suit is a spy and it's alive. That's why some parts of it has to be transparent and not covered in paint - to be allowed to breathe. Notice, how these transparent parts are still kinda reddish. This is due to a web of tiny capillaries, which again proves the theory. Anyway, jokes aside, I find this extremely annoying, because this suit has no impact on Rebuild other than just cosmetic. In fact, I think it was designed that way only for fanservice purposes and doesn't need any research into it. What do you think about it?

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue Nov 16, 2010 8:29 am well yeah, obviously the new test suit was created mainly for fan sābisuuu~ and so fat chicks have another reason to cosplay but regarding point one. i was under the impression the reason it's so transparent is to have less resistance and actually achieve easier synchro rates since the user is not used to it yet. the numerous electrodes are probably there for the overview on vital functions and those antennas on the wrists seem to be some kind of transmission device.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Nov 16, 2010 9:06 am Well, it's a TEST suit. Perhaps it's mean to give Asuka less assistance (Think of the naked test in ep. 13)

[]: Re: Asuka's new test plugsuit.             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Nov 16, 2010 11:41 am DarthGendo wrote: Anyway, jokes aside, I find this extremely annoying, because this suit has no impact on Rebuild other than just cosmetic. In fact, I think it was designed that way only for fanservice purposes and doesn't need any research into it. What do you think about it?

[]: Re: Asuka's new test plugsuit.             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Nov 16, 2010 6:56 pm DarthGendo wrote: 2. Transparent material is used as a mean of visual observation of the pilot's body for abnormalities. First, what kind of abnormalities could emerge and why would someone expect them?

[]:             Quote Post by Naminator » Tue Nov 16, 2010 10:22 pm I'm going to lean towards the obvious fanservice. I mean even Asuka goes "don't you think the new suit is a bit revealing?" or something of that nature. And gentleman, we have been served.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Nov 17, 2010 12:14 am Technology sure did progress quickly in between Mari's "test" suit and Asuka's

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Wed Nov 17, 2010 4:16 am 'Service, of course. Well, I can't come up with any other reason that makes more sense, at least. DarthGendo wrote: 3. This theory is pure genius. Too bad it was created for lulz and trololo. Here it goes. It seems, Angel was residing in the core of Eva-03. How did he turn up inside the entry plug? Wait a minute... Plug suit, of course! Rei was eaten by Zeruel, but never got infected. Asuka did. Why? Plug suit! It all makes sense now. In fact it IS Bardiel. This son of a ***** is like a cacoon around victims body. This plug suit is a spy and it's alive. That's why some parts of it has to be transparent and not covered in paint - to be allowed to breathe. Notice, how these transparent parts are still kinda reddish. This is due to a web of tiny capillaries, which again proves the theory.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 17, 2010 4:24 pm Theory number three is now canon.

[]:             Quote Post by Naminator » Wed Nov 17, 2010 4:47 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Theory number three is now canon.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 18, 2010 3:08 pm http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/Canon

[]: Re: Asuka's new test plugsuit.             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Nov 19, 2010 5:29 pm DarthGendo wrote: What do you think about it?

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Fri Nov 19, 2010 5:39 pm well, let's hope there will never be a male version of this thing. who am i kidding, this is evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:05 pm Fireball wrote: never be a male version

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:08 pm i know, i've seen them. *shudder*

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Nov 19, 2010 8:31 pm Unless the staff explains the exact purpose of the suit and the intent of its design, and why it had to be that revealing, for now we can only surmise it as for the sake of "service". What else? For testing purposes, the head clips are bigger and have more brain hemisphere coverage than the standard clips she often wears. The size ensures maximum coverage, to get as much brain signals to synch while testing.

[]: Re: Asuka's new test plugsuit.             Quote Post by skikes » Fri Nov 19, 2010 10:09 pm DarthGendo wrote: Kaworu didn't wear a gay plugsuit.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 20, 2010 3:14 pm There's nothing inherently derogatory about using the term "Gay" as a descriptive term. Especially when it comes to the idea of giving Kaworu a homoerotic fanservicey Plugsuit.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 22, 2010 1:09 pm Warren Peace wrote: Technology sure did progress quickly in between Mari's "test" suit and Asuka's

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 22, 2010 1:24 pm Fanservice, fanservice, fanservice and fanservice I rather like the aesthetic look it has going for it, though
